inputs.text: Meri tabiyat thik nahi hai, bukhar hai, sirdard hai aur thakawat mehsoos ho rahi hai

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Low-grade fever and malaise are common in viral fever

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Headache and fatigue can also be seen in gastroenteritis

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Although possible, typhoid typically presents with more specific symptoms

inputs.text: Mere bukhar roz badh raha hai, pet dard ho raha hai aur kabz ki samasya hai

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Abdominal pain and constipation suggest a gastrointestinal issue

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Fever can still be part of a viral infection

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Step-wise increase in fever and abdominal pain could be indicative of typhoid, but not conclusive yet

inputs.text: Ab mere bukhar tez ho gaya hai, kabz ke baad dast ho gaya hai aur mujhe bahut kamzori mehsoos ho rahi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: The combination of high fever, transition from constipation to diarrhea, and severe weakness, along with swollen lymph nodes, strongly suggests typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: While gastroenteritis can cause similar gastrointestinal symptoms, the severity of the fever and the presence of swollen lymph nodes are less typical

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Malaria can cause high fever, but the presence of gastrointestinal symptoms and swollen lymph nodes are not typical for malaria

inputs.text: Mere halat aur bhi kharab ho gaye hain, bukhar abhi tak hai, thakawat bahut zyada hai aur doctor ne kaha hai ki intestinal perforation ka khatra hai
inputs.image_desc: prescription and blood test report shown

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: The progression of symptoms, including prolonged high fever, severe weakness, and the risk of intestinal perforation, confirms the diagnosis of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the severity and progression of symptoms not typical for gastroenteritis

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the clinical presentation and laboratory findings not consistent with malaria
